content.problemStatement:
You have an array AAA of integers of size NNN, an array BBB ( initially empty ) and a stack SSS ( initially empty ). You are allowed to do the following operations :


Take the first element of array AAA and push it into SSS and remove it from AAA.
Take the top element from stack SSS, append it to the end of array BBB and remove it from SSS.
You have to tell if it possible to move all the elements of array AAA to array BBB using the above operations such that finally the array BBB is sorted in ascending order.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains a single integer NNN.
Second line of each testcase contains NNN **distinct** integers : A1,A2...ANA_1, A_2 . . . A_NA1​,A2​...AN​.


Output Format
For each testcase, if it possible to move all the elements of array AAA to array BBB using the above operations such that finally, the array BBB is sorted in ascending order, print "YES" (without quotes), else print "NO" (without quotes).
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2*10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of array A. The second line contains N distinct integers A1, A2, ..., AN separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to obtain a sorted (ascending) array B using the allowed operations, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case can be transformed to [1,2,3,4] using the described operations. The second test case cannot produce a sorted array because the element 2 is blocked by larger elements in the stack.

hidden.label: general case sortable with later push

solution.approach: For each test case sort a copy of the input array to obtain the required output order S. Keep an index i pointing to the next element of S that must be produced. Scan the original array from left to right, pushing each element onto a stack. After each push, while the stack is non‑empty and its top equals S[i], pop the stack, increment i and continue. At the end, if i equals N all elements could be output in sorted order, otherwise it is impossible. The algorithm is greedy and works because the only way to output the smallest remaining element is to pop it from the stack as soon as it becomes reachable.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case for sorting, plus O(N) for the stack simulation. Overall O(T * N log N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the sorted copy and the stack.
